message:
feat(dns): implement real DNS transport over UDP/TCP for record lookups and custom nameservers

Replace Phase 0 stubs with a working DNS wire-protocol implementation
that handles MX, SRV, TXT, and custom-nameserver IP lookups.

DNS error (src/net/dns/error.rs):

  Add DnsError::Protocol variant for DNS packet framing and parsing
  errors (malformed labels, truncated packets, invalid header fields).
  Display and test coverage updated.

DNS resolver (src/net/dns/resolver.rs):

  System resolver fast path preserved: when no custom nameservers are
  configured, lookup_ip() continues using std::net::ToSocketAddrs so
  search-domain semantics remain faithful to the host environment.

  Custom nameserver path: when ResolverConfig::nameservers is non-empty,
  lookup_ip() delegates to do_lookup_ip_with_nameservers() which sends
  DNS queries directly to the configured servers. The previous hard-error
  ("custom nameservers in Phase 0 resolver") is removed.

  Record-specific lookups (MX, SRV, TXT) now use a real DNS transport:
  - Wire-format query builder: build_dns_query() constructs a standards-
    compliant DNS query packet with proper header, QNAME encoding, and
    EDNS0 OPT record for larger UDP responses
  - UDP transport with TCP fallback: send_dns_query_sync() sends over
    UDP first; if the response has the TC (truncated) bit set, retries
    over TCP with length-prefixed framing
  - Response parser: parse_dns_response() validates header fields
    (QR bit, RCODE, question count) and extracts answer RRs
  - Record data parser: handles A (1), AAAA (28), MX (15), SRV (33),
    TXT (16) with proper name decompression via parse_dns_name()
  - Retry logic: configurable retry count with per-nameserver rotation
  - effective_nameservers(): when no explicit servers configured,
    discovers system nameservers from /etc/resolv.conf on Linux/macOS
    or falls back to well-known public resolvers

  Helper types: DnsQueryType, DnsRecordData, DnsAnswerRecord for
  structured query/response handling.

  Tests added for: MX lookup, SRV lookup, TXT lookup, custom nameserver
  IP lookup, hostname validation for record lookups, system nameserver
  discovery, query type mapping.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -14,6 +14,8 @@ pub enum DnsError {
     Timeout,
     /// I/O error during DNS query.
     Io(String),
+    /// DNS packet framing or parsing error.
+    Protocol(String),
     /// Connection failed.
     Connection(String),
     /// Operation was cancelled.
@@ -32,6 +34,7 @@ impl fmt::Display for DnsError {
             Self::NoRecords(host) => write!(f, "no DNS records found for: {host}"),
             Self::Timeout => write!(f, "DNS operation timed out"),
             Self::Io(msg) => write!(f, "DNS I/O error: {msg}"),
+            Self::Protocol(msg) => write!(f, "DNS protocol error: {msg}"),
             Self::Connection(msg) => write!(f, "connection error: {msg}"),
             Self::Cancelled => write!(f, "DNS operation cancelled"),
             Self::InvalidHost(host) => write!(f, "invalid hostname: {host}"),
@@ -63,21 +66,25 @@ mod tests {
             DnsError::NoRecords("example.com".into()),
             DnsError::Timeout,
             DnsError::Io("broken pipe".into()),
+            DnsError::Protocol("bad label".into()),
             DnsError::Connection("refused".into()),
             DnsError::Cancelled,
             DnsError::InvalidHost("???".into()),
             DnsError::ServerError("SERVFAIL".into()),
+            DnsError::Protocol("truncated packet".into()),
             DnsError::NotImplemented("AAAA"),
         ];
 
         let expected_display = [
             "no DNS records found for: example.com",
             "DNS operation timed out",
             "DNS I/O error: broken pipe",
+            "DNS protocol error: bad label",
             "connection error: refused",
             "DNS operation cancelled",
             "invalid hostname: ???",
             "DNS server error: SERVFAIL",
+            "DNS protocol error: truncated packet",
             "not implemented: AAAA",
         ];
